prompt: A 19th-century naturalist botanical illustration showing a plant in two states. On the left, a plant grows constrained in a cracked clay pot with shallow roots. On the right, the same plant is shown in cross-section with roots expanding deeply and intricately into the soil, reaching toward a lowest point. Fine engraved linework, muted sepia tones, scientific botanical plate style, detailed root systems, subtle texture of aged paper. No modern elements.
tradition_approach: Fine engraved linework, muted sepia tones, scientific cross-sections, and detailed root structures to mirror naturalist botanical plates while encoding optimization through downward root expansion.
